content: George Eby conducted a study showing that 200 to 500 milligrams of magnesium per meal reduced chronic anxiety, depression, alcoholism, smoking, and sleeplessness.
Magnesium sits on the NMDA receptor in the synapse between neurons, guarding it from triggering stress responses.
Magnesium prevents the NMDA receptor from being triggered by stressors, thus reducing stress and cortisol levels.
Cortisol increases fat storage and affects decision-making, potentially rendering the hippocampus less effective over time.
Eating magnesium-rich foods or taking supplements can break the cycle of chronic stress and improve overall health.
